Farn Polrrrcar- PRACTTcES CouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95g14_2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322_0886
March 2,2007
Dennis Green
5069 Auburn Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92407
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.I-07-006
Dear Mr. Green:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post govemmental
employment provisions of the Political Rgform Act (the "AcF;.r ai"";; y"" Lirr" rrot provided
any facts related to a specific appearance before or cornmunication withr.;;;revious
government agency employer, we are treating your request as one for inf:ormai assistance.2 This
letter should not be construed as assistance on any conduct that mayharre ulreudy taken place. In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair political practices commission
("commission') does not act as a finder ora"t when it renders assistance. -ii; ," oglesby (1g75)
FPPC Ops. 71.)
As a former employee of the california Department of Transportation (,.caltrans,), what
limitations does the Act impose on you while *orkirrg for Lim & Nascimento Lngineering corp.
("LAN') on projects involving Caltrans?
QUESTION
CONCLUSION
As a former state govemment employee, you are subject to the Act's post governmental
employment restrictions as discussed below,
I Gov"rnme.rt Code sections g1000-91014
18997, of the California Code ofRegulations.
Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 1 g 109_
2 rnformal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 1g329(c),,opy 
"n"tor"a.; 
--
tuiM
::


File No. I-07-006
Page No. 2
FACTS
You retired from Caltrans as a Staff Ser"vice Manager I, Specialist in construction. you
were in this position from February 2005 until Septanber 22,20o6-your duties while "*ptoy"a
with caltrans incl rded building support groups throughout Dstrict g, which encompassed
Riverside and san Bernardino counties. you racilitated public information meetings, established
and chaired citizen advisory committees, attended press conferances, and responded to media
and elected official's inquiries regarding on-going freeway construction projects. you also
advised upper management, senior and resident engineers and the public^afllirs office ,"g*oirg
sensitive public issues- You responded to complaints regarding construction projects and
developed and implemented mitigation strategils with communities for construction projects.
In February of 2006, you asked for assistance with setting up meetings and additional
clerical and office support. You needed support staff for your offici because you were in the
field so often' To help with these needs yo,., *"." given four task orders for consultant support in
February of 20a6' Two task orders were with caliop, one was with parsons Engineering Group,
and one was with Harris and Associates. You did noi sit on the selection panels or have any
involvement in the selection process for these task orders and were not the contract manager.
You outlined the tasks each consultant would be performing for you as well as the skills needed
to succeed. The consultants came on board between epril and June of 2006 and worked with
you for about six months until you retired on September 22,2006. This was your only
involvement with consultants throughout your 24-year career with Caltrans.
You began working for LAN as aRisk Manager for Program project Management on
october 2,2006. LAN has a contract with caltrans 6r construction Mairagement and program
Project Management support services, which includes risk management. These contracts were
alteady in place while you were working for Caltrans, and you had no involvement with them
while you were in construction at Caltrans.
During our telephone conversation you stated that you were not a designated employee
under the Caltrans conflict of interest code during your employment as Staffdervices Manager I,
Specialist in construction. However, you also inticated that while working with caltrans you
were responsible for making determinations related to community outreach-and relations. you
1'::],11^""ed 
that your superiors would rely on such determinatiLns when making their final
oeclslons.
- 
For example you described a situation in which Caltrans had marked a few trees to be cut
down near the highway for safety reasons. Manbers of the local community, who were opposed
to eliminating the trees, chained themselves to the trees to prevent Caltrans from cutting them
down' To solve this problem you devised a plan to work with the local elementary school to
have sfudents plant trees along the highway as part of a "Caltrans Day', in e*chunge for allowing
caltrans to cut down the trees without opposition. Your superiors relied on your investigation,
judgment, and decisionmaking ability when they gave approval for this plan.


File No. I-07-006
Page No. 3
when you retired, the four task orders that you were task manager for were rewritten to
another task manager' You want to know if you are precluded from wo'rking for Caltrop,
Parsons Engineerin E, and Harris Engineering on other task orders with Caltrans District g.
ANALYSIS
Public officials who leave state"service are subject to two tlpes ofpost-govemmental
employment provisions under the Act,3 colloquially known as the;i"rotrrirrg doot', prohibitions.a
We look to these provisions to answer your questions.
o Permanent Ban: The first restriction is the'permanent ban" prohibiting a former state
employee from "switching sides" and participating, for compensation, i, any specific
proceeding involving the State of Califomia or assisting others in the proceeding if the
proceeding is one in which the former state employ"" purti"ipated while *rproi"Juvlrr"
state (Sections 8740 I - 87 402, regulation lS7 4l .l).
The permanent ban is a lifetimeban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial, or other
proceeding in which you participated while you sirved as a state adminiskative officiai.l 
---^
"'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceefile' means any proceeding, application, request for a
ruling or other determination, contract, claim, controversy, investigaiio^.r, 
"t *g", accusation,
arrest or other particular matter involving a specific pmti or parties in any court or state
administrative agency . - .-" (Section 87400(";.; aaaitionally, an official is considered to have
"participated" in a proceeding if he or she took part in the proceeding ..personally and
substantially through decision, approval, disappioval, formal written recommendation, rendering
advice on a substantial basis, investigatiorr, oi.rs" of confidential information . . ..,, (Section
87400(d).)
"The permalent ban does not apply to a 'new'proceeding even in cases where the new
proceeding is related to or grows out of a prior proce"airg in which the official had participut"a.
A llew' proceeding not subject to the permanent ban typ[ally involves different parties, a
different subject matter, or different factual issues fromihose considered in previous
proceedings." (Rist Advice Letter, No. 4-04-1g7; see also Donovan Advice Letter,
No. I-03-119.)
3 In addition, section 87407 prornbits certain state and local officials from making, participating in making,
or using their official position to influence decision-s affecting persons yrth whgm they areinJgotiating employment,
or has any arrangement concerning employment. (section sliol ; regulation rg747 .)J 
-- - --- t
a A comprehensive discussion of the elements of the two laws is set forth in the attached .,Leaving your
State Job? Post-Employment Restrictions May Affect You." We have enclosed this document for your information.
We do not repeat this information in the body of this letter.
5 You were a state adminiskative official because your former position meets the definition under section
87400(b) for a state adminishative official. Section 8740a(q defines state administrative official as ..every member,
officer, employee or consultant of a state adminiskative ug"o"y who as part ofhis or her official responsibilities
engages in anyjudicial, quasi-judicial or otherproceeding in other than a purely clerical, secretarial or ministerial
capacity."


File No. I-07-006
Page No. 4
You have not provided any information as to your participation in any proceeding while
employed as a Staff Services Manager I, Specialist inConstruction at Caltrans that may affect
your ability to engage in any of the conduct listed herein. To apply the permanent ban to your
situation, you need to determine if any of the actions in which yor ro- *ish to engage on behalf
of your new employer involve a proceeding in which you partitipated while ernployed at
Caltrans. (Regulation 187 41. I (a)(a).)
o One-Year Ban: The second restriction is the "one-year ban', prohibiting a state
ernployee from communicating for compensation, with his or her former agency for the
pu{pose of influencing certain administrative or legislative action or influen-cinjcertain
proceedings. (Section g7 406, regulation 1 g7 46.1 .t
The one-year ban applies to ernployees who are designated in their former agancy,s
conflict of interest code. However, the one-year ban also uppti"s to employees who made or
participated in the making of governmental decisions that naa a rrrorrity foreseeable material
effect on any financial interest. In other words, the one-year ban applies to former employees
who should have been designated in their former employer's code. isection s7a06(d)(1);
regulation 1 87 a6.1 (a)(2).)
A state employee "makes a govemmental decision" when acting within the scope of his
or her authority, he or she: 1) votes on a matter;2) appoints a person; 3j obhgates his or her
agency to any course of action; 4) enters into any contractual ugr""*rrt on b-ehalf of his or her
agency; or 5) determines not to act, unless the determination is made due to a conflict of interest.
(Regulation 18702.1.)
Additionally, a state employee "participates in making a govefirmental decision,, when he
or she negotiates (without significant substantive review) witt a lorernrnent entity or private
person regarding a govefirmental decision, or when he or she advlses or makes recofirmendations
to the decisionmaker (either directly or without significant substantive;"*), by conducting
research or an investigation or by presenting any report, analysis or opinion, *fri"t ,"qri.", ifr"
exercise of independent judgment on the gart of the ernployee and the outcome or purpose of
which is to influence the decision. (Regulation lS7O2.;.)
we have advised that an employee participates in the making of a governmental decision,
even if it is reviewed by several of his or her rrp"riorr, if any of the-follo#ng appty: l) the
superiors rely on the data or analysis prepared by the employee without checfing it
independently;2) the superiors rely on the professional judgment of the employee; or 3) the
employee in some way actually influences the final decisi on. (LilyqursrAdvice Letter,
No. M-96-318 [Memorandum to the Attorney General].)
Nothing in your facts indicates that your position was properly not designated in the
Calkans conflict of interest code.6 In fact, the current noticed ediiion of the p.opor"a caltrans
6 we note that-caltrans is in the process of updating its conflict of interest code. unless you can provide
documentation from calkans that your position was properly not designated in its code, it appears that your position
should have been designated while you were employid withcalta.rs,"*d you should ,'ulaJfv *rr" one-year ban.


File No. I-07-006
Page No. 5
conflict of interest code now lists all Staffservices Manager I positions in District g, including
your former position, as designated positions. From the facts ihut you have provided, it appears
that although yo, may not have had.final approval of your decisions, you had a significant role in
advising your superiors on the decisions thiy made in connection with your job duties and
actions' Based on these facts provided, it appears that yourposition u, stuffs"*ices Manager I,
Specialist in Construction should have been designatedin Caltrans' conflict of interest code]and
that you are a public official subject to the or"-y"u. ban. Accordingly, the one-year ban will
apply for 72 months from the date you left state employment. (Section g7406(d)(l); regulation
18746.1 (b)QD?
The one-year ban applies to appearances or communications, for which you are
compensated made on behalf of any person as an agent, attorney, or representative. (Section
87406(d)(l); regulations 18746.1(bX3) and (a).) Fiom the facts provided, you are working as a
Risk Manager for Program Project Management for LAN. Since you are'a'paidernployeeif
LAN, you will be working for compensation and in representation of anoth"rporor.
Appearances and communications are prohibited only if they are made for the ..purpose
of influencing" an, "administrative or legislative action" or'Uiscretionary act involvi"g tfr"
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, g;ant:or contract, or the sale
or purchase of goods or property." (Section 87406(dxl); regulatio i tu+a:6)(5).) Regul"ti;;
18746'2 provides that an appearance or communication is foi the purpos" oii"ir""""irrgfrit i,
made for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influen"i"g, *oait rr, oppo.i.rg,
delafng, or advancing the action or proceeding-
Finally, appearances and communications are prohibited if they are before a state agency
the public official worked for or represented, or before a state agency whose budget, p".roir"l,'
and other operations are subject to the control of a state ag"r"y th" public official worked for or
represented. (Section 87406(d)(l); regulation 18746.1(Uliol.l Since you left a position with
Caltrans, the one-yearban applies to any appearance or communications before caltrans,
including all Caltrans districts and employees.
We point out, however, that not all communications are prohibited by the one-year ban.
Appearances or communications before a former state agency employer, made as part of
"services performed to administer, implement, or fulfill ihe requirernlnts of an existing permit,
license, grant, contract, or sale agreement may be excluded from the fo""-v"urt prohibitions . . .
provided the services do not involve the issuance, amendment, awarding, o, revocation of any of
these actions or proceedings." (Regulation 18746.1(bX5XA); euiring,fd,ni"" Letter, No. A-03-
272; Hanan Advice Letter, No. I-00-209.)
7 we have advised that the one-year ban commences when the employee is no longer under an
employment agreement and is no longer receiving compensation, including compensation 6ritrrrr"d,, vacation
time. (Weil Advice Letter, No. A-97-247.)


File No. I-07-006
Page No. 6
AdditionallS regulation 18746.2(bX1)-(4) provides that appearances or communications
are not restricted under the one-year ban, if an individual:
"(1) Participates as a panelist or formal speaker at a conference or similar
public event for educational purposes or io disserninate research and the
subject matter does not pertain to a specific action or proceeding;
"(2) Attends a general informational meeting seminar, or similar event;
"(3) Requests information concerning any matter ofpublic record; or
"(4) Communicates with the press.,,
Finally, the commission has advised that a former agencyofficial may draft proposals on
a client's behalf to be submitted to the agency so long as the form". *ptoy"J is not identified in
connection with the client's efforts to influence administrative action. (colt xvice Letter, No.
A-95-321; Harrison Advice Letter, No. A-92-289.) Simil ar_ly, arorme" ugin"yofficial may use
his or her expertise to advise clients on the procedural requirement*, pr*r] oi policies of the
official's former agency so long as the "*pioy"" is not identified witir trre ernproyer,s efforts to
influence the agency. (Perry Advice Letter, No. 4_94_004.)
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
-$,tLl.,oV.W^,-
SBjgl

By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
